ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: recommendationservice shows the same default SA automount anti-pattern—batch fix it with the other ms-demo Python services so one Helm/Kustomize change clears several alerts.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Policy text and violation messages align: default SA tokens mounted though recommendation ranking rarely needs raw API access.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Stolen workload credentials could abuse Kubernetes API permissions inherited from the SA binding.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Isolated to recommendationservice unless default SA can reach unexpected resources via broad Roles.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: Same MEDIUM risk class as peer services; score reflects operational efficiency of fixing it in a group rather than unique exploit urgency.
recommended_actions: Apply the same automount=false patch used for sibling microservices in the demo.
If gRPC clients need metadata only from ConfigMaps, migrate off in-cluster API reads.
Track remediation in one ticket covering all bookstore Python deployments.
mitigation_strategies: Use admission policies to flag future Deployment creates that leave automount defaulted on in production namespaces.
